sink_vftable_anchor.role: Concrete vfptr root for the long-lived settings-entry sink object previously bounded at record +0x48.
sink_vftable_anchor.evidence_source: Fresh headless no-analysis qword-table and instruction-window recovery on rs2quick32..35.

evidence.observation: the constructor/reset path writes fixed root 0x140BA0630 into the sink object before continuing broader queue/list initialization.

GhidraReadQwordsPy resolves a contiguous code-pointer table rooted at 0x140BA0630, with executable/function targets at successive qword slots and concrete handlers at offsets later consumed by the queue dispatcher.

bounded_inference.statement: 0x140BA0630 is the live sink-object vfptr target, not just an unrelated descriptor root. The adjacent qword at 0x140BA0628 also points at code, so the vfptr root sits inside a broader contiguous code-pointer table rather than a standalone RTTI-labeled blob.

dispatcher_slot_matrix.caller_role: Pending-entry queue pop-and-dispatch routine over sink +0x3A8.

evidence.observation: calls 0x14067BB80 with R8D = 1 and the entry payload at RDX +0x8, proving this slot selects a specific dispatch kind before sink-side handling.
forwards the entry into 0x14067DDA0 with sink +0x3C8 as the working subobject.
on the successful branch, loads [RSI +0x3C8], seeds callback 0x14067CB50 and fixed root 0x140B98DD8, then calls 0x1406CC2B0.
on the failure branch, stores 5 into entry +0x258.
takes lock sink +0x8E8 and forwards the entry into sink +0x938 through 0x140680640.

slot_entries.role: Dispatch-kind 0 path with wildcard/string normalization over the staged entry payload.

evidence.observation: calls 0x14067BB80 with R8D = 0, showing this slot selects a distinct dispatch kind from the +0x48 and +0x58 siblings.
checks sink byte [this +0x28A] before continuing deeper normalization logic.
materializes entry text through 0x1407A11C0 from entry +0x8 into a local string object.
compares the materialized string against literal 0x140B9CD84 = '*' using explicit string-length and buffer normalization logic.

bounded_inference.statement: This slot is a string-sensitive dispatch branch, likely classifying or normalizing wildcard-bearing settings entries before the later sink-side route is chosen. The downstream sink effect lies outside the recovered window.

evidence.observation: calls 0x14067BB80 with R8D = 2 and the entry payload at RDX +0x8, making this the kind-2 sibling of the +0x48 path.
forwards the entry into 0x14067F2B0 with sink +0x3C8 as the working subobject.
on the failure branch, stores 5 into entry +0x258, matching the same terminal marker used by the +0x48 path.
takes lock sink +0x8E8 and forwards the entry into sink +0x938 through 0x140680640, mirroring the same commit sink used by +0x48.

bounded_inference.statement: The +0x48 and +0x58 handlers are sibling staged-publish routes differentiated mainly by the kind selector passed into 0x14067BB80 and the choice of middle helper 0x14067DDA0 versus 0x14067F2B0.

slot_entries.role: Broad sink reset/flush routine over multiple embedded containers and global state bytes.

evidence.observation: starts by forwarding sink +0x3C8 into 0x140680580 and then clearing sink +0x138 through 0x1407A2670.
writes zero to globals 0x140C57946 and 0x140C57947 before continuing the reset path.
tears down and reinitializes the container rooted at sink +0x420, restoring self-pointers and clearing adjacent state.
takes lock sink +0x290, then moves on to the list/container rooted at sink +0x2E0.

bounded_inference.statement: This slot is not just a single-entry dispatcher. It is a full sink reset or flush path that clears multiple queues/maps plus associated globals before continuing deeper into the object.

slot_entries.role: First half of a paired toggle/check path over sink subobject +0x400/+0x408 and status byte +0x8D8.

evidence.observation: bails out early when sink byte +0x8D8 is already nonzero.
works against sink +0x408 / +0x400, seeding fixed root 0x140B98DC8 and a size-like value 0x80 into 0x1406D2730.
if the returned code is 0x65, sets sink byte +0x8D8 = 1 and zeroes EAX.
tail-jumps into 0x14067C120 with RCX = sink and R8D = resulting status code.

slot_entries.role: Second half of the paired toggle/check path over sink subobject +0x410/+0x418 and status byte +0x8D8.

evidence.observation: mirrors the +0x68 handler's opening guard, but only proceeds when sink byte +0x8D8 is nonzero.
works against sink +0x418 / +0x410, using sibling fixed root 0x140B98DD0 and the same 0x80 size-like seed into 0x1406D2730.
if the returned code is 0x65, clears sink byte +0x8D8 = 0 and zeroes EAX.
tail-jumps into 0x14067C120 with RCX = sink and R8D = resulting status code, matching the same post-check sink path as +0x68.

bounded_inference.statement: The +0x68 and +0x70 handlers form an explicit paired toggle/check family that flips sink byte +0x8D8 while routing through sibling embedded resources and sibling literal roots 0x140B98DC8 / 0x140B98DD0.

adjacent_non_dispatch_slot.role: Adjacent sink vftable handler not currently proven to be used by the queue dispatcher.

evidence.observation: consults global 0x140E579E8 and prepares callback-style arguments from local buffers plus sink-side working objects.

constructor_mode_roots.observation: Both constructor-mode roots share the same compact qword layout and do not decode as inline ASCII strings at their first pointed substructures.

bounded_inference.statement: These roots are small static mode/config descriptors selected by R14D in the constructor path, not human-readable labels.

updated_model.statement: The settings-entry sink is now bounded as a concrete vfptr-backed object with a live dispatch matrix at 0x140BA0630. The queue popper at 0x14067B892 routes pending entries into six proven slot targets at +0x48..+0x70, covering two staged publish kinds, one string-sensitive normalization path, one broad sink reset/flush path, and one paired toggle/check family over sink byte +0x8D8.

relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-settings-sink-queue-dispatcher: Closes the earlier placeholder for anonymous vtable slots by naming the exact slot addresses and target functions behind +0x48/+0x50/+0x58/+0x60/+0x68/+0x70.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-settings-publish-sink-container: Extends the earlier sink/container model by showing the sink is a real C++-style vfptr object with distinct publish, reset, and toggle handlers rather than a generic list holder.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-settings-entry-factory-and-publish: Clarifies the downstream publication target reached from 0x140679A30: later queue dispatch does not fan out arbitrarily, but enters this fixed six-slot handler matrix.

next_targets: Recover the full downstream effect of the +0x50 handler after its wildcard/string normalization block so the string-sensitive branch can be named as precisely as the other five slots.
Name fixed roots 0x140B98DC8, 0x140B98DD0, and 0x140B98DD8 by following their consumers outside the immediate sink handlers.
Classify constructor-mode roots 0x140C41920 and 0x140C41A50 by tracing the first consumer of their pointed substructures at 0x140C41948 and 0x140C41A78.
